---
title: Influence of Female Education on Fertility Rate across Countries
format: dashboard
autor: Fayomi Abigail Oludayo 
jupyter: python3
execute:
    echo: false
dashboard:
    expand: false
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px


education = pd.read_csv("data/female_education.csv")
education = education.rename(columns={"name": "country"})
education = education.melt(
    id_vars=["geo", "country"],
    var_name="year",
    value_name="education"
)
education["year"] = pd.to_numeric(education["year"], errors="coerce")
education["education"] = pd.to_numeric(education["education"], errors="coerce")
education = education.dropna(subset=["year", "education"])
education["year"] = education["year"].astype(int)


fertility = pd.read_csv("data/fertility_rate.csv")
fertility = fertility.rename(columns={"name": "country"})
fertility = fertility.melt(
    id_vars=["geo", "country"],
    var_name="year",
    value_name="fertility"
)
fertility["year"] = pd.to_numeric(fertility["year"], errors="coerce")
fertility["fertility"] = pd.to_numeric(fertility["fertility"], errors="coerce")
fertility = fertility.dropna(subset=["year", "fertility"])
fertility["year"] = fertility["year"].astype(int)


population = pd.read_csv("data/population.csv")
population = population.rename(columns={"name": "country"})
population = population.melt(
    id_vars=["geo", "country"],
    var_name="year",
    value_name="population"
)
population["year"] = pd.to_numeric(population["year"], errors="coerce")
population["population"] = pd.to_numeric(population["population"], errors="coerce")
population = population.dropna(subset=["year", "population"])
population["year"] = population["year"].astype(int)


df = education.merge(
    fertility,
    on= ['geo', 'country', 'year'],
    how= 'inner'
)
df = df.merge(
    population,
    on= ['geo', 'country', 'year'],
    how= 'inner'
)
df = df[(df['year']>=1960) & (df['year']<= 2020)]

fig1 = px.scatter(
    df,
    x="education",
    y="fertility",
    size="population",
    hover_name="country",
    animation_frame="year",
    title="Education vs Fertility Over Time",
    labels={
        "education": "Female Education (Years)",
        "fertility": "Fertility Rate"
    }
)


filtered_df = df[df["year"] == 2007]
fig2 = px.scatter(
    filtered_df,
    x="education",
    y="fertility",
    size="population",
    hover_name="country",
    hover_data={"population":":,.0f"},
    title=f"Education vs Fertility in {2007}",
    labels={
        "education": "Female Education (Years)",
        "fertility": "Fertility Rate"
    }
)
education_trend = df.groupby("year", as_index=False)["education"].mean()
edu = px.line(
    education_trend,
    x="year",
    y="education",
    title="Average Female Education Over Time"
)


fertility_trend = df.groupby("year", as_index=False)["fertility"].mean()
fert = px.line(
    fertility_trend,
    x="year",
    y="fertility",
    title="Average Fertility Over Time"
)


selected_year = 2007
filtered_df = df[df["year"] == selected_year]
top_education = filtered_df.nlargest(10, "education")
top_edu = px.bar(
    top_education,
    x="education",
    y="country",
    orientation = 'h',
    color_discrete_sequence=["skyblue"],
    title=f"Top 10 Countries by Female Education in {selected_year}"
)
top_edu.update_layout(height=600)
top_edu.update_yaxes(categoryorder = 'total ascending')


top_fertility = filtered_df.nlargest(10, "fertility")
top_fert = px.bar(
    top_fertility,
    x="fertility",
    y="country",
    orientation = 'h',
    color_discrete_sequence=["skyblue"],
    title=f"Top 10 Countries by Fertility in {selected_year}"
)
top_fert.update_layout(height=600)
top_fert.update_yaxes(categoryorder = 'total ascending')


selected_year = 2007
filtered_df = df[df["year"] == selected_year]
avg_education = filtered_df["education"].mean()
avg_fertility = filtered_df["fertility"].mean()
total_population = filtered_df["population"].sum()

countries_to_compare = ["Nigeria", "Germany"]

compare_df = df[df["country"].isin(countries_to_compare)]

compare_edu = px.line(
    compare_df,
    x="year",
    y="education",
    color="country",
    title="Education Comparison Over Time"
)

compare_fert = px.line(
    compare_df,
    x="year",
    y="fertility",
    color="country",
    title="Fertility Comparison Over Time"
)

avg_education = round(filtered_df["education"].mean(), 2)
avg_fertility = round(filtered_df["fertility"].mean(), 2)
total_population = round(filtered_df["population"].sum() / 1e9, 2)
```



# HOME

This dashboard analyzes the relationship between **female education**, **fertility rate**, and **population** across countries from **1960** to **2020**.

Using global development data, the analysis explores how increasing access to education may influence population growth and demographic change.


## ROW {height="30%"}

::: {.valuebox icon= "heart" color="#1E90FF" title="Average education"}

`{python} str(avg_education)`

:::

::: {.valuebox icon= "heart" color="#7e77e4" title="Average fertility"}

`{python} str(avg_fertility)`

:::

::: {.valuebox icon= "heart" color="#1efff4" title="Total population"}

`{python} str(total_population)`

:::

## ROW {height="70%"}

### {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig1
```


### {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig2
```

Countries with higher levels of female education tend to have lower fertility rates. 
This suggests a strong negative relationship between education and the number of children per woman.


# TRENDS

## Row {height=50%}

### {width="50%"}

```{python}
edu
```

### {width=50%}

```{python}
fert
```


### {width=20%}

Over time, female education has steadily increased globally, while fertility rates have declined. 
This pattern supports the idea that improved education is associated with lower fertility.


# Rankings

## Row 1 {height=50%}
```{python}
top_edu
```


## Row 2 {height=50%}

```{python}
top_fert
```



# Yearly Trend

## Row 1 {height=70%}

### {width="50%"}

```{python}
compare_edu
```

### {width="50%"}

```{python}
compare_fert
```


# Country comparison

## Row {height=60%}

### Column {width="80%"}
```{python}
fig1
```


# ABOUT

Data source: Global development indicators dataset (education, fertility, population).
Visualization built using Python, Pandas, Plotly, and Quarto.
